_comment: Autonomous original-post decision-maker (src/triggers/ai_manager.py) -- no reposts, those are manual-only now. Deliberately a lighter, quality-over-volume profile: max_posts_per_day lowered to 9 (was 24) after observing that a near-hourly cadence read as a wall of posts rather than a curated, worth-following source -- the same instinct that already kept reposts sparse. Still a ceiling, not a target: Claude can (and should) post fewer on a quiet day. Each Claude call still produces a BATCH of up to posts_per_batch posts -- the first fires immediately, the rest queue and drain one per subsequent hourly run -- but the daily cap now binds much sooner in the day, which is intentional: it pushes real selectivity rather than filling every available hourly slot. CONTEXT-tagged posts (routine market-recap/evergreen filler) are explicitly deprioritized in the prompt in favor of genuinely newsy JUST IN/BREAKING/NEWS/CRYPTO/AI content -- the account's real purpose is informing people of what's actually happening, not padding a post count. No images and no links, by deliberate choice -- second_part_every_n_posts nudges Claude's own per-post second_part decision (a genuine continuation posted as a reply, roughly 1 in second_part_every_n posts) instead; the account's own profile is meant to be enough to inform a reader with no outbound clicks needed.
